title: Chassalia ridleyi
taxonomy: Chassalia ridleyi is a species of plant within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. This family comprises over 600 genera and 13,000 species, many of which are found in tropical regions. Within the Rubiaceae family, Chassalia ridleyi is classified as a member of the genus Chassalia, which is a small genus of flowering plants native to Asia. The exact classification of Chassalia ridleyi can vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a species within the family Rubiaceae. Chassalia ridleyi is a flowering plant native to Borneo.

intro: Chassalia ridleyi, also known as Ridley's Chassalia, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Bioactive constituents isolated from the plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional medicine to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
